title: Chapter 5 (intermediate) — The airflow recipe & TV spatialization
hero.title: Under the hood of the wind
hero.lead: The sound of wind, seen as a signal graph: pink noise through a lowpass filter, a single value driving everything, and a stereo pan that gives the heading. And on the TV side, how to place a sound in space.
s1.h2: The recipe for a gust of air
s1.p1: The base is pink noise (gentler than white noise, it “sounds” more like air) run through a lowpass filter. The stronger the wind, the wider the filter opens and the higher the volume — the air becomes both more present and brighter.
s1.p2: On top of that base we layer, as needed, a resonance (a singing bandpass) for the howl, or gusts driven by slow oscillators. Four assemblies give four characters.
s2.h2: Adapt to strength, reveal the heading
s2.p1: A single value drives everything: the game's wind, from −95 to +95 px/s². Its magnitude (0 to 1) opens the filter, raises the volume and deepens the gusts; its sign gives the direction (positive = rightward).
s2.p2: To hear that heading, the sound leans across the stereo field toward the side the wind blows (the stronger it is, the more it leans). And in “directional gust” mode, a gust travels across your headphones left to right (or the reverse), swelling as it passes: you can't mistake the ear.
s2.callout.nerd: For the curious. Direction is set with a single StereoPannerNode: pan = sign · strength, from −1 (hard left) to +1 (hard right). The travelling gust simply animates that pan over time.
s3.try: Put on headphones to hear left/right.
s3.p1: The TV convention: left/right via stereo pan (X position), front-to-back via the depth volume, and up-down via a slight timbre tilt — brighter up top, deeper down low. Pick a sound, then click the battlefield to emit it at that position.
s3.cfgCap: validated config (paste back into the game)
